title: jacobaea maritima subsp. bicolor
intro: Jacobaea maritima subsp. bicolor, also known as sea ragwort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the daisy family that is native to the coastal areas of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antitumor effects. The health benefits of Jacobaea maritima subsp. bicolor include its ability to aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as arthritis, gout, and skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
